exemplar.title: To what extent were Liberty Media's strategic initiatives effective in increasing the popularity and growth of Formula 1?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent were Liberty Media's strategic initiatives effective in increasing the popularity and growth of Formula 1?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear framing of Liberty Mediaâs initiatives in terms of change
Consistent linkage of strategic moves to organisational outcomes
Definitions of change provided in several sections
weaknesses: Lacks an academic definition up front to anchor analysis
Some evaluative comments (e.g., cost cap critique) treat finance in isolation rather than as change
Concept of change drifts into descriptive narrative in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the investigation demonstrates strong understanding of 'change' and connects it clearly to Liberty Mediaâs actions (matching band-4 descriptors), integration is not sustained through every evaluative point and an academic definition is missing. This places the work at the upper-mid range, meriting a score of 3/5.

strengths: Five supporting documents supplied, satisfying quantity requirements
Documents (Forbes, filings, Nielsen, Statista) are directly relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Limited interrogation of source reliability and range of perspectives
Inconsistent captioning and incomplete reference details reduce depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student provides the required number of relevant documents and includes quantitative evidence, they do not critically assess reliability or offer a broad range of viewpoints. This aligns most closely with band-2 performance in relevance and depth, yielding a score of 2/4.

strengths: Concepts such as rebranding and cost cap are linked to the research question
weaknesses: No formal business tools or frameworks are used
Application of strategic concepts lacks methodological rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA uses very few recognised business tools and rests on generic strategic language without formal frameworks. Relevance to the research question is superficial, placing this work at band-1 for tool selection and application (1/4).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiple quantitative data sets (viewership, revenue, valuations) to support points
Includes some evaluative remarks on limitations (e.g., risks of rebranding, cost cap criticisms)
weaknesses: Analysis is uneven and occasionally descriptive without linking back to strategic initiatives
Figures are sometimes pasted without commentary or critical interpretation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is generally effective, and there is analysis of the impact of each strategy. However, synthesis across strategies is limited and some data lacks interpretation, matching band-3 descriptors (3/5).

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question
Reiterates key findings (viewership growth, revenue increases)
weaknesses: Does not fully integrate earlier acknowledged limitations into final strategic recommendations
Over-generalises success without reconciling all caveats
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions are consistent with evidence and clearly state an answer but lack full reconciliation of earlier limitations. This aligns with band-2 performance (2/3).

strengths: Logical progression from introduction through to conclusion
Comprehensive table of contents and clear section headings
weaknesses: Key terms like âpopularityâ and âgrowthâ are not operationally defined
Some data tables include unrelated information that could distract
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate and coherent structure throughout, satisfying band-2 descriptors (2/2).

strengths: All required IB elements are present: title page, word count, contents, supporting document list, works cited
Table of contents is well-structured and aids navigation
weaknesses: OCR artifacts interrupt narrative flow in places
Citation formatting is inconsistent across sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met, despite minor formatting/OCR issues. This corresponds to band-2 performance for presentation (2/2).

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical sequence with a table of contents, section headings and clear progression from introduction to conclusion, fulfilling the âappropriate structureâ requirement.

Documents (Forbes, Liberty Media filings, Nielsen, Statista, etc.) are clearly relevant and provide useful quantitative evidence.  Depth is satisfactoryâfinancial tables, graphs and survey data are includedâyet the range of viewpoints is limited and the student rarely interrogates reliability.  This aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depth,â not the highest level that demands extensive depth and range.
All IB-required elementsâtitle page with word count, contents, supporting documents list and works citedâare present.  Thus the top band for presentation is met.
Five supporting documents are supplied, meeting the rubric requirement of 3-5 sources.  This places the work in the mid-upper range for the strand.

Where concepts are used they are linked superficially to the research question (e.g., quoting revenue growth after each initiative).  Application lacks methodological rigour and often drifts into narrative description, so relevance is present but shallow, matching the âsuperficial relevanceâ descriptor.

Multiple quantitative data sets (viewership charts, revenue tables, team valuations) are incorporated and referenced.  The selection is sufficient in breadth and generally used to support points, though occasional figures are pasted without commentary.  Overall usage is effective enough for the second-highest band.

The student analyses the impact of each strategy and includes some evaluative remarks about limitations (e.g., risks of rebrand, criticism of cost cap).  However, analysis is uneven and at times descriptive, and the synthesis across strategies is limited.  This corresponds to âsome analysisâ / âmostly effectiveâ rather than sustained, thorough integration.

The research question is explicitly answered in the final section, asserting that the initiatives were effective while noting some caveats.  The response is clear and direct, satisfying the highest descriptor for this strand.

The IA employs very few recognised business tools or theories; there is no SWOT, PEST, Ansoff, or Porter.  Discussion rests on generic strategic concepts (rebranding, cost cap) rather than formal frameworks.  This reflects a âlimited selectionâ band.

The conclusion reiterates findings and cites supporting evidence (viewership rise, revenue growth), showing partial consistency.  Yet it over-generalises success without fully reconciling earlier limitations, so evidence alignment is unevenâappropriate for the mid-band.

Throughout the commentary the student explicitly connects each strategic move to Liberty Media and Formula 1, citing viewership, revenue and valuation data from the organisation.  These links are analysed rather than merely stated, showing how the initiatives affected F1âs popularity.  The analysis is persuasive but not wholly integrated across every paragraph (some narrative passages drift into description).  Hence the strand sits most comfortably in the âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ band.

The student firmly anchors the investigation in the key concept of change, repeatedly linking Liberty Mediaâs strategic actions (re-branding, cost-cap, broadcasting reforms) to organisational transformation. Definitions are provided and each major section is framed as a change initiative, demonstrating strong conceptual understanding.  However, the notion of change is not woven consistently through every evaluative comment (e.g., cost-cap critique is treated largely as finance rather than as further organisational change).  This partial but not total integration aligns with the âstrong understanding with partial integrationâ descriptor.

annotations.comment: The Ecclestone quote is relevant but lacks critical commentary on its impact. Evaluate how shifting from expert judgment to data-driven tactics affected outcomes.
The research question is clear and focused, but key terms like âpopularityâ and âgrowthâ would benefit from operational definitions to guide consistent analysis.
The focus concept âChangeâ is stated but lacks a concise definition here. Introduce an academic definition to frame all subsequent analysis.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, facilitating navigation through sections and supporting documents.
The methodology description omits details on how the four documents were selected and the criteria used to assess their relevance. Clarify selection criteria.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Introduction cites supporting documents 2 & 3 but the commentary later refers to five. Ensure the document count is accurate and consistent.
The rebranding definition is generic. Consider applying a formal strategic framework (e.g., Kotlerâs brand identity prism) to deepen theoretical grounding.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure 1âs caption describes viewership but stops short of interpreting the trend. Link the observed changes directly to Liberty Mediaâs strategic initiatives.
The evaluation notes rebranding risks but does not tie these limitations back to measurable growth or popularity metrics. Strengthen the critique with data.
The broadcasting section ends mid-sentence, disrupting coherence. Review PDF extraction to ensure the full analysis is presented.
The claim of a 58% audience increase should reference the methodology or sample size for ESPNâs data to validate reliability.
The caption for Fig. 2 omits a date and source for Nielsen data. Include full reference details to strengthen credibility.
The Netflix promotion analysis should explicitly connect audience figures to F1âs popularity KPIs (e.g., social media follow-on growth).
Target audience section effectively shows a strategic shift. Consider integrating more on how this change conceptually influenced Liberty Mediaâs actions.
The section on social media change describes benefits but could cite specific engagement metrics (likes, shares) to support claims.
The parent-company revenue table includes unrelated segments. Focus on Formula One Group to streamline data analysis.
Narrative ends abruptly after â2022 atâ; ensure extraction from OCR captures full sentence.
Cost cap benefits are well explained but lack discussion of possible negative brand perception among traditional fans.
Valuation discussion describes higher team values but should refer explicitly to Fig. 5 to ground claims in the presented data.
Team valuations evaluation notes valid limitations but could expand on how cost cap may stifle R&D innovation for smaller teams.
The conclusion summarizes outcomes effectively but could integrate earlier acknowledged limitations into broader strategic recommendations.
Supporting Document 1 table contains UI elements (âSubscribeâ, âSign Inâ). Remove navigation artifacts to focus on content.
OCR error in Supporting Document 2 (â$8hâ enterprise value). Correct numeric notation to reflect accurate valuation.
The mathematical notation âE_1â in Supporting Document 3 appears incorrect. Replace with descriptive text or proper variable.
Works cited entries display mixed citation formats. Standardize to a consistent style (APA or MLA) for clarity and presentation.
